Iberia Líneas Aéreas España, S.A. (Iberia Airlines Spain English), usually shortened Iberia, largest airline Spain, based Madrid.
years following World large advancement aircraft technology with concomitant increase possibilities rapid connections between international business concerns. Germany many business concerns, particularly Africa South America, still reliant ships land routes reach these. order circumvent Treaty Versailles stipulation that prohibited German aircraft access flying over France, agents Spain explored possibility liaison between Germany Spain. such Deutsche Luft Hansa behalf German government, established general agreement with Spanish government which signed December 1927 authorizing service between both countries.
Iberia, Compañía Aérea Transportes incorporated June 1927 with initial capital investment financier Horacio Echeberrieta Deutsche Luft Hansa million pesetas. company obtained Government authorisation establish commercial daily (except Sunday) connections between Madrid Barcelona. fleet three Rohrbach Roland monoplanes were bought from Deutsche Luft Hansa.
Flight operations started December 1927 with inaugural flights aimed coincide commemorative show Madrid. opening ceremony Carabanchel aerodrome attended Alfonso XIII president Iberia, Horacio Echeberrieta. maiden flight from Madrid left schedule, taking from Madrid 12:30 arriving Barcelona hours later. However Barcelona Madrid flight briefly forced stop Almazán, Soria reduced visibility caused snow. Although this flight 9:00, aiming arrive Madrid midday, delay meant arrived Madrid hours after dignitaries already left.
Passengers front Iberia Breguet Limousine 1931.
Within year, company sponsored government provide postal transport between Madrid Barcelona. Meanwhile, Deutsche Luft Hansa favourably rewarded investment Iberia company when granted regular service between Germany Spain. January 1928 route Barcelona Marseilles Geneva Zürich Stuttgart Leipzig/Halle Erfurt Berlin initiated. July 1928, twin-engined Dornier added fleet. This seaplane used pioneering route between Cádiz Canary Islands. This used test viability postal route South America, extending route Cape Verde Islands.
During dictatorship Miguel Primo Rivera, proposed that aviation companies Spain should combined become general interest public utility. This came into effect early 1928. consequence, Iberia merged into Compañía Líneas Aéreas Subvencionadas S.A. (C.L.A.S.S.A.) together with following other airlines Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.), Compañía Española Tráfico Aéreo (C.E.T.A.) pilot training company Compañía Española Aviación (C.E.A.) airship line Transaérea Colón.
Although Iberia ceased activities 1929, barely eighteen months after having begun operations, name "Iberia" continued registered Daniel Araoz Aréjula. airlines that formed CLASSA, Iberia would only keep existence after merger.
Following proclamation Second Spanish Republic 1931 LAPE (Líneas Aéreas Postales Españolas) company, airline that would replace CLASSA, became established. LAPE began operations April 1932, CLASSA flying under Spanish Republican Flag year.
LAPE operated even during Spanish Civil War, mainly Madrid Barcelona line, although great part planes fleet were requisitioned Spanish Republican Force used military transports.
name "Iberia" still registered, used anew when airline operations began 1937 territory held rebel faction during Spanish Civil War. 1939, after conflict, planes belonging LAPE's fleet were repainted with Iberia airline livery.
When Iberia company fully restarted operations following Civil War, became purely domestic airline. right many international routes, such service between Madrid Lisbon; which been previously established early 1927 Unión Aérea Española (U.A.E.).
Inaugural flight Bata (Ecuatorial Guinea) 1941.
eighth Iberia's capital investment offered Littoria, fascist Italy's airline. After some deliberation, negative experience investing related Corporacion Americana Servicios Aéreos, Littoria decided invest capital Iberia form aircraft which were join Iberia's fleet. Pact Madrid 1953, visa requirements were eliminated visitors Spain. From 1961 Iberia fleet Douglas DC-8 four-engined long range airliners. busiest route Madrid Buenos Aires. 1965 joint board Iberia Aviaco coordinate policies that services conflict. early 1970s company bought Douglas DC-9s Boeing 747s expanded routes Central America, Warsaw, Athens Istanbul.

this time Iberia began build interests other Spanish airlines Aviaco, Viva Air, Binter Canarias Binter Mediterráneo Latin American airlines Aerolíneas Argentinas, Viasa Ladeco. 2002, Iberia celebrated 75th anniversary making oldest airlines world. During those years nearly million people have been flown Iberia, placing company among five largest European airlines, important routes connecting Spain with Europe Latin America.
Iberia Group encompasses Iberia Regional/Air Nostrum franchise. addition transporting passengers freight, Iberia carries many related activities, such aircraft maintenance, handling airports, systems, in-flight catering, holiday packages.
Iberia Group airlines destinations countries. code-sharing arrangements with other companies, offers flights another destinations countries. With fleet almost aircraft, makes about 1,000 flights each day. 2002, Iberia carried million passengers 210,000 tons freight.
addition, Iberia aircraft maintenance company, servicing fleet those another companies, including some European Iberia main supplier aircraft handling services Spanish airports, with more than airline clients.
Iberia founding partner computerised ticket reservation system Amadeus CRS, with 18.28% stake this sold 2005. also tour operator through Viva Tours Tiempo Libre units, with Cacesa supplies package delivery services.
Iberia allied with American Airlines British Airways, September 1999, joined Oneworld alliance. British Airways owns share capital.
July 2004, Iberia announced decided move Latin American from Miami, Florida Pedro Sula, Honduras. Although rumoured plans that airline considering Luis Muñoz Marín International Airport were discarded with announcement, Iberia announced that plans expand Puerto Rican schedules connect Juan with Central American cities.
2005, Iberia introduced Business Plus Class, Airbus A340 aircraft.
February 2006 Terminal Madrid Barajas awarded Iberia Oneworld alliance members. This provided expansion capabilities Iberia. Iberia alone responsible around Madrid Barajas traffic. 2005 Iberia regional branch Nostrum transported 21,619,041 passengers to/from Madrid Barajas alone.
November 2009, Iberia Airlines confirmed that reached preliminary agreement merge with British Airways. merger between carriers will create world's third-largest airline terms revenue. April 2010, confirmed that British Airways Iberia agreed merger, forming International Airlines Group, although each airline would continue operate under current brand. International Airlines Group, established head office London registered office Madrid, operating mainly from main hubs London Heathrow Airport Madrid Barajas Airport with secondary hubs London Gatwick Airport Barcelona Prat Airport.
December 1948 Douglas DC-3 crashed weather near Gandesa killing occupants.
March 1973, people were killed mid-air collision above French city Nantes involving Iberia Douglas DC-9 flying from Palma London Flight 504; Convair Coronado aircraft, operating Spantax Flight 400. Spantax Convair able make successful emergency landing whilst Iberia DC-9 crashed killing everyone board. accident occurred during French traffic controllers' strike.
December 1973, Iberia McDonnell Douglas DC-10 registered EC-CBN overran runway upon landing Boston Logan after flight from Madrid Airport. There were fatalities amongst people board, however aircraft written off.
February 1985, Iberia Flight 610, Boeing 727-256 crashed after hitting television antenna installed summit Monte while landing Bilbao, killing people.
July 1996 Iberia Flight 6621, DC-10-30 flying from Madrid Havana hijacked mid-flight. hijacker, Lebanese national named Ibrahim Saada demanded flight diverted Miami International Airport. Saada later apprehended faced years prison. hurt flight later landed Jose Marti International Airport some hours later.
November 2007 Iberia Airbus A340-600, registration EC-JOH, badly damaged after sliding runway Mariscal Sucre International Airport. injuries were reported. According Airbus, aircraft written-off.
